notes: Aviari fighter building toward a dive-attack style that his flight ability won't unlock until level 5. The halberd is chosen for reach and dramatic visual impact. The plan is fully formed. The timeline is aspirational. At level 5 the Aviari flight ability activates — the build is now fully operational. The dive-attack style is no longer aspirational.
personality: I occasionally refer to myself in the third person, usually when announcing a maneuver. The maneuvers have names. I am aware this is a lot and I stand by it.
ideals: The sky belongs to those willing to use it. Staying on the ground is a choice, and it is the wrong one.
bonds: Someone once told me that a ground-bound fighter relying on a polearm was a contradiction in terms. I have been thinking about it ever since. They will understand eventually.
flaws: The concept is significantly further along than the execution. I have committed to a fighting style my body will not fully support for some time yet, and I fill the gap with a confidence that occasionally outpaces the evidence.

time_to_shine.summary: Sagittarius earns his reputation outside of combat through altitude — as a scout, a lookout, and an early warning system the party didn't know they needed. His Skywatcher training means he reads the sky the way other people read a map. The third-person announcements are a separate skill. He has not been asked to stop.

moments.context: The party needs to scout ahead without being seen
moments.text: Sagittarius goes up. At 20–30 feet, he is functionally invisible to ground-level threats and can cover terrain the party would spend an hour crossing in minutes. WIS 12 and Perception proficiency are enough to spot an ambush, a patrol route, or a structural weakness before anyone else knows to look for it. He narrates the report in third person. The information is accurate.

moments.context: The party needs to know what the weather will do
moments.text: Skywatcher of Holmea background. Sagittarius reads cloud formation, wind direction, and pressure shifts the way other people read signage. He can tell the party whether the storm is two hours out or six, whether the pass will be crossable by morning, and whether the fog is natural. He is almost always right. He mentions that he is almost always right.

moments.context: Someone needs to reach a ledge, rooftop, or elevated position that isn't accessible by climbing
moments.text: He flies there. Athletics proficiency handles whatever happens on arrival. He has carried party members to positions they couldn't reach themselves, dropped a rope from above, and once landed on a moving cart from fifteen feet up. That one has a name. He has used the name in three subsequent conversations.

moments.context: The party enters a new city, outpost, or wilderness area for the first time
moments.text: Sagittarius goes up first. He circles once, maybe twice, and comes back with a working mental map of sight lines, entry points, and anything that looks wrong from above. He delivers this as a formal aerial assessment. The party has learned to let him finish before asking questions. The questions are usually answered in the assessment.

combat_tactics.role: Aerial reach controller, fully operational at level 5. Flight is live. Extra Attack is live. A halberd at 10-foot reach swung twice per action from above is exactly what the maneuvers were always named after.

tactics.text: Aviari flight activates at level 5. Sagittarius can now move vertically, hover above the engagement, and descend on targets. Fly up 15–20 ft, then dive to reach with the halberd (10-ft reach = effective attack range from 10 ft above). Enemies on the ground cannot easily follow. Ranged attackers become the priority threat to manage.

Two halberd attacks per action. STR 17 (+3) + prof +3 + Great Weapon Fighting = +6 to hit, 1d10+3 per attack (reroll 1s and 2s on damage dice). Two attacks per turn from above, threatening a 10-ft circle on the ground below. Opportunity attacks now cover a 10-ft radius — no enemy walks away from him for free.

The named move: gain altitude (15+ ft, ideally 20 ft). Dive on the highest-value target within reach. Attack twice. If Action Surge is available: four halberd swings in one dive pass. This is the sequence that had a name before it was possible. Now it is possible. The name was right.

Skybound Captain bonus action: give a tactical order to an ally within 30 ft — they gain advantage on their next attack or +10 ft movement. Use when Sagittarius is repositioning mid-air and cannot swing productively that turn. The captain role is not cosmetic — it applies real advantage where it matters most.

Aerial position is powerful but creates exposure to archers and ranged casters. Maintain 20+ ft altitude where possible: melee threats are locked out, and most archers are at disadvantage. When a ranged enemy is the priority, descend, close with Great Weapon Fighting, and remove them with two attacks before returning to altitude.
